notes: This manifest is the source of truth for candidate selection, internal review state, and approved UI attachment.
Only entries with ui_attachment.approved=true should render in the user-facing UI.

Current shipped UI only treats outfit, awkward, and size posts as primary outfit-preview candidates; buy and review posts must not remain ui_attachment.approved=true unless the UI eligibility rule changes.

posts.scene_context: weekday office elevator mirror before a morning meeting
posts.style_concern: the trousers may break too sharply above loafers and make the outfit feel shorter
posts.intended_tone: believable office outfit, understated commute styling
posts.review_notes: Approved after privacy-variation refresh. Uses a non-black phone cover while keeping the lower-half proportion question readable.
posts.prompt: Create one realistic outfit preview image for a Korean mobile-first fashion community post. This is a styling preview, not an exact virtual try-on and not official product photography. Scene context: weekday office elevator mirror before a morning meeting. Style concern: the trousers may break too sharply above loafers and make the outfit feel shorter. Intended tone: believable office outfit, understated commute styling. Keep the image believable for a real Korean everyday social-fashion upload. Prefer half-body or full-body vertical mobile framing with a candid mirror selfie, hallway check, elevator mirror, apartment entryway, office mirror, or plain daily-life backdrop. If the scene is outdoors, keep it to an ordinary Seoul sidewalk or office-adjacent corner without scenic or campaign styling. Use this privacy treatment: phone-covered face with a dark navy or graphite phone, not pure black, while keeping the lower half fully readable. Vary privacy treatment naturally across the set instead of defaulting to the same black-phone face-cover composition. Use ordinary indoor or overcast daylight and visible everyday imperfections such as slight grain, faint mirror smudges, or natural garment wrinkles. Make the outfit feel like weekday office, commute, lunch-plan, or after-work styling rather than an editorial campaign. Show the exact debate point clearly in frame so the viewer can judge proportion, fit, balance, or awkwardness from the image itself. Use the reference product images only as guidance for silhouette, category, and color family. Treat product references as secondary guidance only, not as the main subject of the image. Do not add any text overlay, collage layout, luxury-campaign polish, dramatic pose, cinematic angle, or catalog symmetry. Do not imply exact brand-official proof, real ownership history, durability proof, or exact garment transfer.

generation.revised_prompt: Create one realistic, vertical smartphone photo for a Korean mobile-first fashion community post: a candid elevator mirror selfie in a Seoul office building before a morning meeting. Subject’s face is fully covered by a graphite or dark navy phone (not pure black), held at mid-face; hair and ears partly visible, but identity hidden. Outfit is believable weekday office/commute styling inspired by the references (use only for silhouette and color family, not exact transfer): a camel wool zip-front jacket with a soft self-belt loosely tied over an ivory ribbed turtleneck; navy wide-leg trousers in sturdy twill; dark brown leather loafers. Show the style debate clearly: keep the lower half fully readable, with the trouser hem creating a sharp break right on the loafer vamp, slight crease and light stacking that makes the legs look a touch shorter; a hint of dark socks peeking. Composition: full-body vertical framing, slightly off-center with a natural handrail and stainless steel elevator walls, faint mirror smudges, small dust flecks, and subtle grain. Ordinary indoor/overcast daylight from the elevator lighting; believable white balance; no dramatic angle, no cinematic color grading, no text, no collage, no brand logos. Add tiny everyday details like a slim tote strap on one shoulder and a work badge clipped at the waist. Keep the scene understated and imperfect so it feels like a real Korean daily-life upload, with the outfit and trouser break clearly judged in-frame.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create one photorealistic, mobile-first vertical (9:16) outfit preview for a Korean social-fashion post. Candid elevator mirror selfie in a modest Seoul office building on a weekday morning. Stainless elevator walls, soft fluorescent lighting with a hint of overcast daylight from the lobby, faint mirror smudges and slight film grain. No text, no collage, no branding, not a campaign shot.

Subject: a young woman taking the selfie with her phone fully covering her face; hair in a low ponytail. Full body visible, natural stance with one foot slightly forward so proportions and hem length are clear.

Outfit styling (inspired by references for silhouette/color only, not exact products):
- Light sky-blue tweed short-sleeve mini dress with subtle texture, clean round neckline, tiny faux chest-pocket seams, and a slim self belt; A-line skirt hits mid-thigh with mild natural wrinkles near the waist.
- Over it, a dark chocolate-brown, collarless, lightly structured cropped jacket with long sleeves, hidden placket, gentle shoulder volume; worn open to soften the formality.
- Black leather loafers with thin black socks.
- Simple small off-white shoulder bag worn casually, or a beige canvas tote tucked under arm to suggest commuting (choose one bag, understated, no logos). Minimal jewelry.

Mood and intent: weekday outside-appointment office look that risks reading too formal for a normal morning; the brown cropped jacket + loafers + practical bag should dress it down. Keep everything believable for a real Korean daily post—ordinary lighting, slight imperfections, natural mirror perspective. Single image only; show the full silhouette clearly so viewers can judge the balance and whether the look still feels too formal.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create a single, realistic mobile-vertical photo for a Korean mobile-first social fashion post. Not a virtual try-on, not product photography. Scene: weekday morning elevator mirror of a modest Seoul apartment building, stainless walls with faint scuffs, cool overhead lighting. Slight natural grain and a few light mirror smudges for everyday feel.

Framing and privacy: candid full-body mirror selfie. The top edge of the mirror trims most of the forehead and the left edge cuts off part of one cheek so the face isn’t fully visible. Phone held low at chest, not covering the entire face. No dramatic angle or editorial polish.

Outfit: believable office-commute look in all black. 
- Top: lightweight charcoal/black V‑neck knit sweater (inspired by the provided reference for silhouette and color family; do not copy exact details or logos). Subtle sleeve wrinkles at the elbows. Slight front‑tuck to show waistline. 
- Bottom: black tailored straight‑leg trousers, clean lines with a natural crease and a soft break at the shoe. 
- Shoes: black leather loafers with a gentle shine, everyday wear. 
- Bag: structured black leather boxy shoulder/satchel bag with long slim handles (use the second reference only for general shape and color; no exact brand markings).

Debate point emphasis: keep the outfit entirely black except for ONE balancing accessory that is clearly visible—tie a slim pale taupe silk twilly/scarf in a tidy knot around one handle of the black bag. Make this single accent understated but noticeable enough to judge balance versus the monochrome outfit.

Additional realism: a slim stainless watch, minimal; soft natural wrinkles at the knees; elevator floor indicator blurred in the background, no readable logos or text. Ordinary morning vibe as if heading to work or lunch plans. No text overlay, no collage, no cinematic lighting, no symmetrical catalog pose. Keep it believable for a real Korean everyday social-fashion upload.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create a single photorealistic vertical 9:16 outfit preview image for a Korean mobile-first fashion community post. Not a virtual try-on and not official product photography—make it look like a candid weekday mirror selfie after arriving at the office in Seoul.

Scene: elevator lobby/elevator mirror in a modern office building. Stainless steel panels, warm gray tile floor, soft overhead fluorescent light mixed with faint daylight from the hallway. Slight phone grain, natural white balance, faint mirror smudges, and mild garment wrinkles for realism. No text, no collage, no branding, no campaign polish.

Framing: full-body vertical mirror selfie. Phone held naturally and slightly off-center so the face is only partially covered (eyes and part of forehead visible), neutral expression. Everyday UGC silhouette, relaxed stance with one foot slightly forward. Include the phone in frame. Keep background believable for a Seoul office (subtle Korean office vibe, e.g., elevator control panel, card reader, or hallway glimpse), but no readable brand logos.

Subject: a realistic young Korean office worker (20s–30s), average build and height. Anonymous, non-celebrity, natural hair tied back or loosely down. Minimal makeup, understated jewelry. Casual weekday energy (commute/lunch-plan mood).

Outfit to clearly show the styling debate: “oversized upper half may make the body look heavier than expected.” Emphasize proportion so viewers can judge it.
- Top: oversized, boxy charcoal-gray blazer with dropped shoulders and slightly long sleeves covering part of the hands. Under it, a loose light-blue oxford shirt; front casually half-tucked to reveal the waistband, back untucked. Buttons simple, collar relaxed.
- Bottom: high-waisted camel/tan pleated wide-leg trousers with a long break touching the shoes, similar in silhouette and color family to the provided reference image but not an exact copy or brand-identical. Soft drape with front pleats.
- Shoes: black square-toe low heels or sleek loafers.
- Accessories: slim black leather belt (subtle), simple silver hoops, black shoulder tote or laptop bag on one shoulder; optional slim office ID clip visible at the waistband.

Direction: capture from a slightly low, straight-on angle common to mirror selfies so the top’s width reads clearly, accentuating the volume across the shoulders and chest while the trousers fall smoothly—making the proportion debate obvious. Keep the pose natural, not editorial. Ensure a believable everyday UGC feel with slight mirror smudges and minimal motion blur on the phone hand.

Output: one single realistic image, vertical smartphone framing, no added text or graphics.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create a single realistic vertical mobile photo for a Korean fashion community post. Scene: weekday hallway/elevator mirror check in a Seoul apartment building right before heading out for work. Lighting: ordinary cool indoor daylight from ceiling panels; slightly flat, no dramatic shadows. Camera: candid mirror selfie, full-body vertical framing, phone held low around chest so the face is visible with a soft natural-looking blur (privacy treatment should feel incidental, not templated). Add subtle everyday imperfections: faint mirror smudges, mild phone-grain, small wrinkle at the sleeve and skirt crease, a tiny scuff on the shoe, and a slightly crooked composition. No text overlay, no collage, no brand logos anywhere. Style/tone: believable everyday social fashion upload, not editorial, not catalog, not a virtual try-on.

Outfit details to show the debate point clearly: 
- Top: light-blue oxford button-down tucked in, sleeves casually rolled once. 
- Outerwear: slim beige trench worn open. 
- Bottom: medium-gray A-line midi skirt in wool suiting; hem hits mid-calf. 
- Shoes: black chunky leather penny loafers that read a bit heavy; pair with thin off-white socks to help viewers judge whether the lower half feels too heavy versus the skirt. 
- Accessories: slim black belt, small black shoulder bag. 

Pose/composition: subject stands relaxed with one foot slightly forward and angled so the loafers’ bulk is clear in the reflection; compose to include the entire skirt length and both shoes with a bit of floor space. Keep proportions honest so viewers can assess balance/fit. Background: plain apartment elevator interior with stainless panels and muted beige floor tiles; maybe a glimpse of hallway through the open door—ordinary Seoul apartment vibe. Keep everything natural and believable, no dramatic angles, no symmetry, no campaign polish. Generate one image only.

generation.revised_prompt:
Create one realistic vertical 9:16 mobile mirror-selfie outfit preview for a Korean fashion community post.

Scene and mood:
- Small Seoul clothing-store fitting room or office dressing-room; beige/gray walls, a wooden bench, a metal hook with a simple tote or umbrella; slightly cramped feel.
- Ordinary indoor lighting with a bit of cool fluorescent cast and faint daylight spill; no studio polish. Slight mirror smudges and a touch of phone-camera grain.
- Weekday try-on before purchase; believable everyday vibe, not an editorial campaign.

Framing and privacy:
- Half-to-full body framed vertically in the mirror.
- The mirror crop naturally cuts off the top of the head and most of the face (eyes not visible). The phone is held low at chest/torso level—do not use a deliberate phone-to-face cover. No text overlay, no collage, no dramatic angles.

Outfit (use the provided black blazer photo only as loose guidance for silhouette and color; do not copy logos or exact brand details):
- Black tailored 2-button blazer, notch lapels, light structure in the shoulders, mid-hip length. Slight natural wrinkles, realistic fabric texture.
- Underneath: off-white crewneck T-shirt tucked in.
- Bottoms: light gray straight trousers, ankle length with subtle break.
- Shoes: clean white leather sneakers.
- Accessories: small black nylon crossbody worn on the shoulder, thin silver watch. Keep accessories understated.

Pose and composition to show the style debate clearly:
- Model stands square to the mirror, one button of the blazer fastened and the other open to emphasize the V-shape and slightly sharp lapels; one hand lightly in a trouser pocket to show proportion, the other holds the phone.
- Ensure the frame includes shoulders down to mid-shin so viewers can judge shoulder structure, blazer length relative to hips, sleeve length at the wrist, and overall balance with the casual tee/sneakers.

Additional realism details:
- Subtle garment creasing at elbows and waist; slight unevenness in the hem; faint scuffs on the floor; neutral color balance.
- Keep the background ordinary and a bit imperfect. No brand signage, no product tags visible, no implication of official product photography.

Deliver a single candid image that feels like a real Korean weekday office try-on/mirror check, focused on whether the black blazer reads too sharp/expensive for daily rotation.

generation.revised_prompt:
One realistic, single-frame outfit preview for a Korean mobile-first fashion community post. Vertical 9:16 smartphone shot. Candid elevator-mirror selfie in a typical Seoul office building on a cold weekday morning—stainless doors, faint smudges on the mirror, cool fluorescent light with soft overcast daylight spill from the hallway. Slight, natural grain. No text overlays, no collage, no logos, no editorial polish.

Privacy treatment: three-quarter mirror angle. The subject turns slightly to the right so only part of the face is visible; medium-length dark hair falls forward to cover the cheek and brow. A slim, muted pastel phone case partially blocks the lower face, held at chest to chin level (not the straight-on phone-cover pose). Eyes are not clearly visible.

Outfit (use reference product only for silhouette/color family, not as exact brand or product): camel wool coat with a zip-front and a thin tie belt. The coat shown here is one size up for an oversized look—drop shoulders fall clearly past the natural shoulder line, sleeves slightly ballooned, body width visibly generous. Belt is untied and hanging to emphasize volume. Underneath: ivory ribbed turtleneck knit. Bottoms: charcoal straight-leg trousers (slim but not skinny) that end just above plain black leather ankle boots. Accessories: simple black leather tote with laptop weight, charcoal knit scarf casually tucked into the tote, thin black gloves peeking from a pocket.

Make the debate (true-to-size vs oversized) obvious in a single image: frame the upper body from head to mid-thigh so the shoulder seams, chest width, sleeve volume, and coat length are clearly visible against the slimmer trousers. Have the subject lightly pinch a small fold of extra fabric at the left side seam near the waist with one hand to hint how a true-to-size or belted fit would reduce width, while the other hand rests by the belt ends. Ensure the shoulder line and sleeve drop are cleanly readable in the mirror.

Mood: everyday commute, realistic and subtle—natural garment wrinkles, slight coat creasing at the elbows, faint elevator-mirror fingerprints, normal hallway reflections. Balanced exposure, soft shadows, no dramatic lighting or cinematic angle. Keep it believable as a regular Korean social-fashion upload, not official product photography.
